methods.name: ClosestPoint(Point3d)
methods.signature: public Point3d ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint)
methods.summary: Finds a point on the brep that is closest to testPoint.

params.name: testPoint
params.doc: Base point to project to brep.

returns.doc: The point on the Brep closest to testPoint or Point3d.Unset if the operation failed.

methods.name: ClosestPoint(Point3d, Point3d, ComponentIndex, Double, Double, Double, Vector3d)
methods.signature: public bool ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out Point3d closestPoint, out ComponentIndex ci, out double s, out double t, double maximumDistance, out Vector3d normal)
methods.summary: Finds a point on the Brep that is closest to testPoint. The method searches all Brep faces looking for the one closest to testPoint. When found, if the closest point falls on the inactive region of the face, then the method finds the face's edge that is closest to testPoint.

params.name: testPoint
params.doc: Base point to project to surface.

params.name: closestPoint
params.doc: Location of the closest Brep point.

params.name: ci
params.doc: Component index of the Brep component that contains the closest point. Possible component index types are ComponentIndexType.BrepEdge and ComponentIndexType.BrepFace.

params.name: s
params.doc: If ci.ComponentIndexType == ComponentIndexType.BrepEdge, then s is the parameter of the closest edge point.

params.name: t
params.doc: If ci.ComponentIndexType == ComponentIndexType.BrepFace, then (s,t) is the parameter of the closest face point.

params.name: maximumDistance
params.doc: If maximumDistance > 0, then only points whose distance is <= maximumDistance will be returned. Using a positive value of maximumDistance can substantially speed up the search.

params.name: normal
params.doc: If ci.ComponentIndexType == ComponentIndexType.BrepEdge, then this is the tangent to the edge at s. If ci.ComponentIndexType == ComponentIndexType.BrepFace, then this is the normal to the face at (s,t).

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded, false otherwise.

methods.name: CreateOffsetBrep(Brep, Double, Boolean, Boolean, Double, Brep[], Brep[])
methods.signature: public static Brep[] CreateOffsetBrep(Brep brep, double distance, bool solid, bool extend, double tolerance, out Brep[] outBlends, out Brep[] outWalls)
methods.summary: Offsets a Brep.

params.name: brep
params.doc: The Brep to offset.

params.name: distance
params.doc: The distance to offset. This is a signed distance value with respect to face normals and flipped faces.

params.name: solid
params.doc: If true, then the function makes a closed solid from the input and offset surfaces by lofting a ruled surface between all of the matching edges.

params.name: extend
params.doc: If true, then the function maintains the sharp corners when the original surfaces have sharps corner. If False, then the function creates fillets at sharp corners in the original surfaces.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: The offset tolerance.

params.name: outBlends
params.doc: The results of the calculation.

params.name: outWalls
params.doc: The results of the calculation.

returns.doc: Array of Breps if successful. If the function succeeds in offsetting, a single Brep will be returned. Otherwise, the array will contain the offset surfaces, outBlends will contain the set of blends used to fill in gaps (if extend is false), and outWalls will contain the set of wall surfaces that was supposed to join the offset to the original (if solid is true).

params.name: geometry

params.name: uSpans
params.doc: The number of spans in the U direction.

methods.name: CreatePatch(IEnumerable<GeometryBase>, Surface, Int32, Int32, Boolean, Boolean, Double, Double, Double, Boolean[], Double)
methods.signature: public static Brep CreatePatch(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, Surface startingSurface, int uSpans, int vSpans, bool trim, bool tangency, double pointSpacing, double flexibility, double surfacePull, bool[] fixEdges, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Constructs a brep patch using all controls

params.name: geometry
params.doc: A combination of curves, brep trims, points, point clouds or meshes. Curves and trims are sampled to get points. Trims are sampled for points and normals.

params.name: startingSurface
params.doc: A starting surface (can be null).

params.name: uSpans
params.doc: Number of surface spans used when a plane is fit through points to start in the U direction.

params.name: vSpans
params.doc: Number of surface spans used when a plane is fit through points to start in the U direction.

params.name: trim
params.doc: If true, try to find an outer loop from among the input curves and trim the result to that loop

params.name: tangency
params.doc: If true, try to find brep trims in the outer loop of curves and try to fit to the normal direction of the trim's surface at those locations.

params.name: pointSpacing
params.doc: Basic distance between points sampled from input curves.

params.name: flexibility
params.doc: Determines the behavior of the surface in areas where its not otherwise controlled by the input. Lower numbers make the surface behave more like a stiff material; higher, less like a stiff material. That is, each span is made to more closely match the spans adjacent to it if there is no input geometry mapping to that area of the surface when the flexibility value is low. The scale is logarithmic. Numbers around 0.001 or 0.1 make the patch pretty stiff and numbers around 10 or 100 make the surface flexible.

params.name: surfacePull
params.doc: Tends to keep the result surface where it was before the fit in areas where there is on influence from the input geometry

params.name: fixEdges
params.doc: Array of four elements. Flags to keep the edges of a starting (untrimmed) surface in place while fitting the interior of the surface. Order of flags is left, bottom, right, top

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Tolerance used by input analysis functions for loop finding, trimming, etc.

returns.doc: A brep fit through input on success, or null on error.

methods.name: Trim(Brep, Double)
methods.signature: public Brep[] Trim(Brep cutter, double intersectionTolerance)
methods.summary: Trims a brep with an oriented cutter. The parts of the brep that lie inside (opposite the normal) of the cutter are retained while the parts to the outside (in the direction of the normal) are discarded. If the Cutter is closed, then a connected component of the Brep that does not intersect the cutter is kept if and only if it is contained in the inside of cutter. That is the region bounded by cutter opposite from the normal of cutter, If cutter is not closed all these components are kept.

params.name: cutter
params.doc: A cutting brep.

params.name: intersectionTolerance
params.doc: A tolerance value with which to compute intersections.

returns.doc: This Brep is not modified, the trim results are returned in an array.

methods.name: Trim(Plane, Double)
methods.signature: public Brep[] Trim(Plane cutter, double intersectionTolerance)
methods.summary: Trims a Brep with an oriented cutter. The parts of Brep that lie inside (opposite the normal) of the cutter are retained while the parts to the outside ( in the direction of the normal ) are discarded. A connected component of Brep that does not intersect the cutter is kept if and only if it is contained in the inside of Cutter. That is the region bounded by cutter opposite from the normal of cutter, or in the case of a Plane cutter the half space opposite from the plane normal.

params.name: cutter
params.doc: A cutting plane.

params.name: intersectionTolerance
params.doc: A tolerance value with which to compute intersections.

returns.doc: This Brep is not modified, the trim results are returned in an array.

properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not the Brep is manifold. Non-Manifold breps have at least one edge that is shared among three or more faces.

properties.name: IsSolid
properties.summary: Determines whether this brep is a solid, or a closed oriented manifold.

properties.name: IsSurface
properties.summary: Returns true if the Brep has a single face and that face is geometrically the same as the underlying surface. I.e., the face has trivial trimming. In this case, the surface is the first face surface. The flag Brep.Faces[0].OrientationIsReversed records the correspondence between the surface's natural parametric orientation and the orientation of the Brep.trivial trimming here means that there is only one loop curve in the brep and that loop curve is the same as the underlying surface boundary.
